generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard 2D/3D LiDAR sensor providing high-resolution spatial mapping and obstacle detection for autonomous vehicle environment perception. Operates with unspecified sampling frequency and data transmission rate via DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEntity interface."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HokuyoLiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelRealsenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel RealSense D435i depth camera providing 3D environmental perception data for autonomous vehicle obstacle avoidance and path planning. Integrated with DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEntity via unspecified manufacturer-specific protocol."
    DTDFVocab:producedData IntelRealsenseDepthData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard sensor suite measuring vehicle state variables including speed, acceleration, and orientation for ACC system monitoring and control. Interfaces with DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEntity for real-time telemetry."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateTelemetry
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Data Transmission
instance HokuyoLiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw 2D/3D point cloud data stream from Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor transmitted via Wi-Fi UDP protocol at high frequency (≥10Hz) for real-time clustering and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance IntelRealsenseDepthData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw depth sensor data from Intel RealSense D435i camera transmitted via Wi-Fi UDP protocol for 3D environmental perception and object detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealsenseD435iDepthCamera
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state data including speed, acceleration, and orientation transmitted via ROS2 middleware for ACC system monitoring and control."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks generated from raw LiDAR/depth data via PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFunctionalEntity for ACC system decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelRealsenseD435iDepthCamera
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARPointCloudModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D point cloud model representing the vehicle's operational environment derived from Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor data. Used for obstacle detection and path planning."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFunctionalEntity
]

instance DepthPerceptionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D environmental perception model constructed from Intel RealSense D435i depth camera data for obstacle avoidance and spatial mapping."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFunctionalEntity
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic model representing the autonomous vehicle's kinematic and dynamic state variables for ACC system simulation and control."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AdaptiveCruiseControlFunctionalEntity
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks data derived from raw LiDAR/depth sensor data through clustering and tracking algorithms. Used for ACC system decision-making and visualization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTracksData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AdaptiveCruiseControlFunctionalEntity, RealTimeVisualizationFunctionalEntity
]

instance VehicleMetricsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Normalized vehicle metrics including speed, acceleration, and orientation data processed through DataTranslationFunctionalEntity for standardized reporting."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCollectingFunctionalEntity
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEntity : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Central entity managing all physical sensor and actuator interfaces using OPC UA and manufacturer-specific protocols. Facilitates bidirectional communication between physical and digital twin components."
]

instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional entity performing real-time point cloud clustering and object tracking from LiDAR/depth sensor data using ROS2 middleware. Outputs structured object tracks for ACC system."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataTranslationFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247 compliant functional entity responsible for data serialization, message alignment, and unit conversion across all data streams to ensure consistency."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Core ACC computation engine offloaded from the physical vehicle to the digital twin. Processes sensor fusion and path planning algorithms with 100ms latency."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataCollectingFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional entity aggregating processed data streams for logging, reporting, and visualization purposes via SQL database backend."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization engine rendering LiDAR point clouds, object tracks, and vehicle metrics in real-time for operator monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based middleware facilitating real-time data acquisition, sensor fusion, and inter-process communication between physical and digital twin components."
]

instance NVIDIAJetsonComputeNode : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson NX/Orin Nano device providing edge computing capabilities for low-latency processing of sensor data and ACC algorithms."
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization service enabling engineers and operators to monitor vehicle state variables, sensor data streams, and system metrics via user interface."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight, SensorDataLoggingAction, ReportGenerationAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service offloading ACC system computations from the vehicle to the digital twin, reducing onboard processing load while maintaining 100ms latency."
    DTDFVocab:provides SensorDataFusionInsight, PathPlanningAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling remote enabling/disabling of ACC system, speed threshold setting, and emergency braking commands via digital twin interface."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCSystemControlAction, ParameterAdjustmentAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D visualization of vehicle state, sensor data streams (LiDAR, depth camera), and system metrics for operator monitoring."
]

instance SensorDataLoggingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated logging of sensor data streams and system metrics for post-analysis of ACC performance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ReportGenerationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generation of performance reports and analytics from logged sensor data for system validation and tuning."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SensorDataFusionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Fused sensor data representation combining LiDAR and depth camera inputs for accurate environmental perception."
]

instance PathPlanningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of path planning algorithms in the digital twin environment for ACC system decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ACCSystemControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote enabling/disabling of the ACC system and execution of emergency braking commands for the physical vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjustment of ACC parameters including time-to-collision thresholds and following distance via digital twin interface."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic speed controller interfacing with the vehicle's motor for throttle control commands received from the ActuationFunctionalEntity."
]

instance VehicleMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electric motor driven by voltage commands from the Vedder Electronic Speed Controller for vehicle propulsion."
]

instance SteeringServo : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Precision steering servo receiving directional commands from the ActuationFunctionalEntity for autonomous vehicle navigation."
]

instance ActuationFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional entity converting user commands and digital twin control signals into actuation commands for the physical vehicle's speed controller and steering system."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C18: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelRealsenseD435iDepthCamera, VehicleStateSensor,
                 VedderElectronicSpeedController, VehicleMotor, SteeringServo
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEntity, PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFunctionalEntity,
                 DataTranslationFunctionalEntity, AdaptiveCruiseControlFunctionalEntity,
                 DataCollectingFunctionalEntity, RealTimeVisualizationFunctionalEntity,
                 ROS2Middleware, NVIDIAJetsonComputeNode
]

instance AutonomousVehicleSystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "1/10-scale autonomous vehicle equipped with Adaptive Cruise Control Digital Twin system."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment AutonomousVehicleEnvironment, DigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this DTS involves control commands generated by the Digital Twin Entity and transmitted to the Physical Twin Entity for actuation. Key aspects include:

- **Command Types**:
  - **ACC (Adaptive Cruise Control) commands** for speed and braking adjustments (explicitly mentioned).
  - **Remote management signals** for system-wide control (implied by 'Controlling FE' but not detailed).

- **Control Message Formats**:
  - Commands are serialized and translated via the **Data Translation FE** (ISO 23247 compliant).
  - Message alignment and unit conversions are performed for compatibility with physical actuators.

- **Validation and Authentication**:
  - Not explicitly detailed in the document. Assumptions about validation (e.g., protocol-level integrity) are not supported.

- **Feedback Systems**:
  - **Vehicle state information** (motor/servo feedback) is transmitted back to the Digital Twin for monitoring (as per **Data Pre-Processing FE** and **Actuation FE**).
  - No explicit mention of closed-loop feedback validation or conflict resolution mechanisms.

- **Conflict Resolution**:
  - Not described in the document. The system does not explicitly address command conflicts or prioritization.

- **Command Execution Confirmation**:
  - Not explicitly detailed. Execution logs or acknowledgment mechanisms are not mentioned.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between all physical and digital components to ensure temporal consistency, as mandated by hardware limitations and safety-critical vehicular application requirements. Network latency is constrained to **at most 100 ms** to maintain real-time coordination between entities. The system leverages ROS2 packages as individual functional execution units, each implementing specific features (FEs) aligned with the ISO 23247 reference architecture. Time-sensitive operations such as pre-processing, actuation, and device communication are implicitly governed by this 100 ms constraint, though finer-grained microsecond-level precision (e.g., inherent to ROS2) is not explicitly documented. The temporal granularity is dictated by the synchronization requirements of the Physical Twin Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity, ensuring alignment between the physical system and its digital counterpart."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adopts an **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)-inspired orchestration** with substantial deviations to align with automotive-specific requirements. The architecture is implemented as a **ROS2-based microservices framework**, where each Function Entity (FE) is deployed as an independent ROS2 package containing ROS2 nodes. Key orchestration patterns include:
  - **Entity decomposition**: Physical Twin Entity (hardware resources + general automotive functions), Digital Twin Entity (data management/control), and User Entity (user-facing components).
  - **ROS2-based coordination**: Topics for real-time data streams (e.g., sensor data, actuation commands), services for request-response interactions, and actions for long-running operations.
  - **Hardware-aware deployment**: Each entity is mapped to distinct hardware components, impacting functionality ownership and engineering processes. The system explicitly notes that deviations from ISO 23247 may affect **ownership of functionality** and **engineering processes** due to the Physical Twin Entity's expanded scope.
  Resource management is implicit in the ROS2 execution model, leveraging the operating system's scheduling and the ROS2 middleware's quality-of-service (QoS) policies for bandwidth, latency, and reliability. The system does not mention cloud-edge orchestration or federated Digital Twin coordination."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of the Digital Twin (DT) system in the context of ISO 23247 is primarily aligned with the manufacturing lifecycle phases where the DT serves as a digital representation of Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs). The standard does not explicitly define a formal lifecycle governance model but describes the DT’s role in **design, operation, and maintenance phases** of manufacturing systems. During the **design phase**, the DT enables virtual prototyping and simulation of OMEs (e.g., equipment, facilities, or processes) to validate performance before physical implementation. In the **operation phase**, the DT continuously monitors and controls OMEs via the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity (DCDC)**, ensuring real-time alignment between the digital and physical domains. The **Digital Twin Entity (DTE)** dynamically updates its representation based on OME observations, though the standard does not specify transition mechanisms or governance frameworks for lifecycle transitions. The lifecycle is implicitly governed by the **entity-based reference architecture**, where each entity (OME, DCDC, DTE, User Entity) operates independently but collaboratively, with no explicit mention of maintenance phases or formal lifecycle management protocols beyond the DT’s role in supporting physical modifications through updated digital representations."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The **ISO 23247** standard defines Digital Twins as requiring **real-time and high-fidelity computational reflections** of physical systems but does not provide **mandatory fidelity requirements**, **validation/verification (V&V) methods**, or **uncertainty quantification** frameworks. The standard explicitly states that **Functional Entities (FEs)** lack implementation guidelines, implying flexibility in model fidelity approaches. No mention of **error detection mechanisms** (e.g., sensor drift correction, model recalibration), **quality assurance (QA) processes**, or **uncertainty propagation** techniques (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations) is found. The only fidelity-related reference is the general principle of **high-fidelity representation**, without technical specifications or correction protocols.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Technical Connection** infrastructure relies on a hybrid communication architecture:

- **Communication Protocols**:
  - **ROS2** for intra-system communication between Software Functional Entities (FEs) within the Digital Twin Entity.
  - **Wi-Fi (UDP)** for inter-entity communication between Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity, ensuring low-latency data exchange.

- **Network Topology and Architecture**:
  - **Star topology** implied by inter-entity communication over Wi-Fi, with the Digital Twin Entity acting as a central hub for processed data.
  - **ROS2 network** for intra-system FE communication (likely a local bus or mesh topology).

- **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
  - **Latency requirement**: ≤100 ms for real-time monitoring and control (explicitly mentioned for ACC).
  - Bandwidth specifications are not provided in the document.

- **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
  - Not explicitly detailed. Assumptions about redundancy (e.g., failover mechanisms) are not supported.

- **Security Protocols**:
  - Not explicitly mentioned. Security assumptions (e.g., Wi-Fi encryption) are not documented.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is described as follows:

- **Hosting Platforms**:
  - **Edge deployment**: Each entity (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User Entity) is deployed on **different hardware components** (not co-located on the vehicle’s onboard computer).
  - No explicit mention of cloud or on-premise deployment.

- **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
  - Not explicitly detailed. Specifications for CPU, GPU, or storage are not provided.

- **Scalability Mechanisms**:
  - Not described. The document does not mention horizontal/vertical scaling or resource allocation strategies.

- **Deployment Models Supported**:
  - **Distributed deployment**: Each entity operates independently on separate hardware (implied by the architecture).
  - No mention of containerization (e.g., Docker) or orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes).

- **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
  - **Latency requirement** (≤100 ms) implies reliability for real-time operations, but no explicit redundancy or failover mechanisms are described.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
    The document explicitly references the **ISO 23247 standard**, which serves as the foundational framework for Digital Twins in manufacturing:

    1. **ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing**:
       - **Scope**: Defines a **development framework** with four parts, with **Part 2 (Reference Architecture)** being the most relevant.
       - **Key Components**:
         - **Entity-Based RA**: Structured around **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, **Data Collection/Control Entities**, **Digital Twin Entities**, and **User Entities**.
         - **Functional Entities (FEs)**: Modular units for specific tasks (e.g., data transformation, user interfaces).
       - **Interoperability**: While not prescriptive, the standard **encourages compliance** with **industry standards** like **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for data exchange.

    2. **Compliance Frameworks**:
       - The standard itself acts as a **certification framework**, though the document does not detail **formal certification requirements** or **audit processes**.

    3. **Industry Standards**:
       - **OPC UA**: Mentioned as a potential implementation choice for FEs, highlighting **interoperability** between systems.
       - **Not in Document**: Lack of details on **other relevant standards** (e.g., IEC 62264 for enterprise integration, ISO 50001 for energy management in DTs).

    4. **Interoperability**:
       - The architecture’s **modularity** and **entity separation** imply **plug-and-play compatibility** with other systems adhering to similar standards.
  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) must adhere to **ISO 15622:2018** performance requirements, ensuring functional safety and reliability in vehicular applications. The system implements **safety-critical ACC functionality** as defined by the standard, with explicit compliance to **ISO 26262-1:2018** for functional safety in road vehicles, though no explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, authentication) is provided.

For **network latency and real-time performance**, the Digital Twin System (DTS) must maintain **≤100 ms end-to-end latency** from the vehicle to the Digital Twin Entity, as derived from **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0 (Release 15)**. This specification mandates **low-latency communication** for V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services, ensuring deterministic behavior critical for safety-critical applications like ACC. The system must also incorporate **fail-safe mechanisms** to mitigate risks associated with latency violations or communication failures, though specific protocols (e.g., time-sensitive networking, redundancy checks) are not detailed in the provided documents.

The Digital Twin architecture emphasizes **real-time monitoring and control** of ACC systems, where safety-critical operations are governed by **hard real-time constraints** (e.g., 100 ms latency) to prevent collisions or unsafe deceleration. While **cybersecurity measures** (e.g., access control, threat mitigation) are not explicitly outlined, the reliance on **ISO 26262-1** implies adherence to safety-integrity levels (ASIL) for hardware/software components, indirectly addressing safety through structured risk assessment. No explicit mention of **data anonymization, consent management, or data sharing agreements** exists in the documents."
]
